-.summary: Broadcasts scalar subtraction from each element of the vector - Parameters: - lhs: The vector - rhs: The scalar value to subtract - Returns: A new vector with the scalar subtracted from each element

!=.signature: static func != (lhs: Self, rhs: Self) -> Bool
!=.summary: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether two values are not equal.

*.summary: Broadcasts scalar multiplication to each element of the vector (commutative) - Parameters: - lhs: The scalar value to multiply - rhs: The vector - Returns: A new vector with each element multiplied by the scalar

\/.summary: Broadcasts scalar division to each element of the vector - Parameters: - lhs: The vector - rhs: The scalar value to divide by - Returns: A new vector with each element divided by the scalar

+.summary: Broadcasts scalar addition to each element of the vector (commutative) - Parameters: - lhs: The scalar value to add - rhs: The vector - Returns: A new vector with the scalar added to each element

accuracy.summary: Fraction of predictions that match the actual labels: (TP + TN) / total.

add.summary: Element-wise addition of two matrices - Parameter other: The second matrix - Returns: A new matrix where each element is the sum of corresponding elements

AggregationMethod.summary: Specifies how values within a group or window are combined into a single result.

AggregationMethod.count.summary: Count the number of values in the group

AggregationMethod.max.summary: Select the maximum value in the group

AggregationMethod.mean.summary: Calculate the arithmetic mean of the group

AggregationMethod.min.summary: Select the minimum value in the group

AggregationMethod.percentage.summary: Sum all values per group, then normalize so all groups sum to 100

AggregationMethod.sum.summary: Sum all values in the group

allSatisfy.signature: func allSatisfy(_ predicate: (Self.Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> Bool
allSatisfy.summary: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether every element of a sequence satisfies a given predicate.

and.summary: Performs element-wise logical AND with another boolean array.

angle.summary: Returns the angle between two vectors in radians.

angleInDegrees.summary: Returns the angle between two vectors in degrees.

arange.signature: static func arange(_ start: Element, _ stop: Element, step: Element) -> [Element] where Element : Comparable
arange.summary: Creates a sequence of evenly spaced values within a half-open interval.

areValidVectorDimensions.summary: Returns whether all vectors in the collection have the same dimension count. Used to validate that a collection of vectors can be used together in mathematical operations.

argmax.summary: Returns the index of the largest element in the array.

argMax.summary: Returns the index of the largest element in the array.

argmin.summary: Returns the index of the smallest element in the array.

argMin.summary: Returns the index of the smallest element in the array.

asFraction.summary: Converts this value to its simplest fractional representation.

asFractions.summary: Converts each element to its simplest fractional representation.

asPercentages.summary: Converts each value to its percentage of the total sum.

averaged.summary: Calculates the element-wise average of a collection of vectors.

bestFit.signature: static func bestFit(data: [[Double]], k: Int, maxIterations: Int = 100, attempts: Int = 10) -> KMeans
bestFit.summary: Fits multiple K-Means models with different seeds and returns the best one.

bikeSharing.summary: 731 rows, 16 columns. Regression. Target column: `cnt` (total daily rides).

broadcast.signature: func broadcast(with value: Element, operation: (Element, Element) -> Element) -> [Element]
broadcast.summary: Broadcasts a custom operation with a scalar value to each element - Parameters: - value: The scalar value to use in the operation - operation: A closure that defines the operation to perform - Returns: A new array with the operation applied to each element

bundledURL.signature: private static func bundledURL(for csvFile: String) -> URL
bundledURL.summary: Resolves a bundled CSV path relative to the sandbox's working directory.

catalog.summary: Returns a human-readable listing of every bundled accessor.

categoricalMappings.summary: Maps an original column name to the ordered class names produced when that column's strings were alphabetically encoded into `Double` indices.

ceil.summary: Returns the ceiling of each element in the array.

centroid.summary: The centroid position for this cluster.

centroids.summary: The final centroid positions after convergence.

choose.summary: Returns a new array with elements conditionally chosen from this array or another - Parameters: - condition: Boolean mask determining which array to choose from - other: The alternative array to choose elements from when condition is false - Returns: Array with elements chosen conditionally from this array or other

classDistribution.summary: Returns the number of samples per class as a dictionary.

classes.summary: The learned statistics for each class, one entry per unique label.

Classification.summary: A group of data points sharing the same predicted class label.

classificationReport.summary: Returns a formatted per-class classification report comparing these predictions against actual labels.

Classifier.summary: A protocol for supervised classification models that predict discrete class labels.

classify.summary: Groups input features by their predicted class label.

Cluster.summary: A single cluster from a K-Means model, containing a centroid and its assigned data points.

clusterCohesion.summary: Calculate average pairwise similarity as a measure of cluster cohesion.

clusters.summary: Groups the original training data into clusters with their centroids.

column.summary: Extracts a column from a matrix at the specified index

compactMap.signature: func compactMap<ElementOfResult>(_ transform: (Self.Element) throws -> ElementOfResult?) rethrows -> [ElementOfResult]
compactMap.summary: Returns an array containing the non-`nil` results of calling the given transformation with each element of this sequence.

compare.signature: func compare<Comparator>(_ lhs: Comparator.Compared, _ rhs: Comparator.Compared) -> ComparisonResult where Comparator : SortComparator, Comparator == Self.Element
compare.summary: If `lhs` is ordered before `rhs` in the ordering described by the given sequence of `SortComparator`s

conditionNumber.summary: Returns the condition number of the matrix using the 1-norm.

confusionMatrix.summary: Builds a confusion matrix comparing these predictions against actual labels.

ConfusionMatrix.summary: Captures the four outcomes of a binary classifier — true positives, false positives, true negatives, and false negatives — and derives standard evaluation metrics from them.

contains.signature: func contains(where predicate: (Self.Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> Bool
contains.summary: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the sequence contains an element that satisfies the given predicate.

correlationMatrix.summary: Calculates the Pearson correlation matrix for multiple data series.

cos.summary: Returns the cosine of each element in the array.

cosineOfAngle.summary: Returns the cosine of the angle between two vectors.

cosineSimilarities.summary: Calculates cosine similarity between each vector in the array and a target vector.

count.signature: func count<E>(where predicate: (Self.Element) throws(E) -> Bool) throws(E) -> Int where E : Error
count.summary: Returns the number of elements in the sequence that satisfy the given predicate.

cumulativeProduct.summary: Returns the running product at each position in the array.

cumulativeSum.summary: Returns the running total at each position in the array.

Dataset.summary: A bundled dataset wrapping a Quiver `Panel` of numeric columns.

DatasetLoader.summary: Single chokepoint that converts a CSV file into a `Dataset`.

denominator.summary: The denominator of the fraction (always positive)

derivative.summary: Computes the numerical derivative by dividing consecutive differences by the sample rate (time between measurements).

describe.summary: Returns per-column summary statistics as a formatted string.

description.summary: Human-readable summary of the dataset's origin, row count, columns, and any cleaning that was applied. For example, the bundled `iris` dataset's description records that it holds 150 rows across three species, four numeric features, and was originally published by R. A. Fisher in 1936.

determinant.summary: Returns the determinant of a square matrix.

diag.summary: Creates a diagonal matrix from a vector of diagonal values.

diff.summary: Calculates the difference between each element and the element a fixed number of periods before it.

distance.summary: Calculates the Euclidean distance between two points or vectors.

DistanceMetric.summary: The distance metric used to compare samples in K-Nearest Neighbors.

divide.summary: Element-wise division of two matrices - Parameter other: The second matrix - Returns: A new matrix where each element is the quotient of corresponding elements

dot.summary: Calculates the dot product of two vectors.

downsample.signature: func downsample(factor: Int, using method: AggregationMethod) -> [Element]
downsample.summary: Reduces the array size by grouping consecutive elements into fixed-size windows and aggregating each window into a single value.

drop.signature: func drop(while predicate: (Self.Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> DropWhileSequence<Self>
drop.summary: Returns a sequence by skipping the initial, consecutive elements that satisfy the given predicate.

dropFirst.summary: Returns a sequence containing all but the given number of initial elements.

dropLast.summary: Returns a sequence containing all but the given number of final elements.

elbowMethod.signature: static func elbowMethod(data: [[Double]], kRange: ClosedRange<Int>, maxIterations: Int = 100, seed: UInt64? = nil) -> [(k: Int, inertia: Double)]
elbowMethod.summary: Computes inertia for a range of k values (elbow method).

elementsEqual.signature: func elementsEqual<OtherSequence>(_ other: OtherSequence, by areEquivalent: (Self.Element, OtherSequence.Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> Bool where OtherSequence : Sequence
elementsEqual.summary: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether this sequence and another sequence contain equivalent elements in the same order, using the given predicate as the equivalence test.

embed.summary: Converts an array of words to their vector embeddings by looking up each word in the embeddings dictionary.

enumerated.summary: Returns a sequence of pairs (*n*, *x*), where *n* represents a consecutive integer starting at zero and *x* represents an element of the sequence.

exp.summary: Returns e raised to the power of each element in the array.

exponentialMean.summary: Computes the exponential moving average, where recent values carry more weight.

f1Score.summary: Harmonic mean of precision and recall: 2 * P * R / (P + R).

falseNegatives.summary: Positive samples the model incorrectly identified as negative.

falsePositives.summary: Negative samples the model incorrectly identified as positive.

featureCount.summary: Number of feature columns the scaler was fitted on.

FeatureScaler.summary: A column-wise min-max scaler for feature matrices.

filter.signature: func filter(_ isIncluded: (Self.Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> [Self.Element]
filter.summary: Returns an array containing, in order, the elements of the sequence that satisfy the given predicate.

filtered.summary: Returns a new panel containing only the rows where the mask is true.

findDuplicates.signature: func findDuplicates(threshold: Double = 0.95) -> [(index1: Int, index2: Int, similarity: Double)]
findDuplicates.summary: Find pairs of vectors with similarity above the specified threshold.

first.signature: func first(where predicate: (Self.Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> Self.Element?
first.summary: Returns the first element of the sequence that satisfies the given predicate.

fit.summary: Fits a scaler, trains a Gaussian Naive Bayes classifier, and bundles them into a Pipeline.

flatMap.signature: func flatMap<SegmentOfResult>(_ transform: (Self.Element) throws -> SegmentOfResult) rethrows -> [SegmentOfResult.Element] where SegmentOfResult : Sequence
flatMap.summary: Returns an array containing the concatenated results of calling the given transformation with each element of this sequence.

flattened.summary: Flattens a two-dimensional matrix into a one-dimensional array by concatenating rows in row-major order.

floor.summary: Returns the floor of each element in the array.

forEach.signature: func forEach(_ body: (Self.Element) throws -> Void) rethrows
forEach.summary: Calls the given closure on each element in the sequence in the same order as a `for`-`in` loop.

formatted.signature: func formatted<S>(_ style: S) -> S.FormatOutput where Self == S.FormatInput, S : FormatStyle

fourierDominantFrequency.summary: Returns the frequency with the highest amplitude in the spectrum, excluding the DC component (0 Hz).

fourierFrequencies.summary: Maps each output bin to its corresponding frequency in Hz.

fourierFrequenciesHalf.summary: Returns only the positive-frequency half of the frequency axis.

fourierInverse.summary: Performs an inverse Fourier transform on real-valued input.

fourierMagnitude.summary: Returns the amplitude at each frequency bin.

fourierMagnitudeHalf.summary: Returns the amplitude spectrum for only the positive-frequency half.

fourierPhase.summary: Returns the phase angle (in radians) at each frequency bin.

fourierSpectrum.signature: func fourierSpectrum(sampleRate: Double, windowed: Bool = false) -> [(frequency: Double, magnitude: Double)]
fourierSpectrum.summary: Returns the positive-frequency spectrum as paired frequency and magnitude values, with automatic padding and optional windowing.

Fraction.summary: A rational number representation for educational display of numeric results.

full.signature: static func full(_ count: Int, value: Element) -> [Element]
full.summary: Creates a 1D array filled with a specific value.

GaussianNaiveBayes.summary: A trained Gaussian Naive Bayes classifier.

GaussianNaiveBayes.ClassStats.summary: Statistics learned from the training data for a single class.

groupBy.signature: func groupBy(_ categories: [String], using method: AggregationMethod) -> [String : Element]
groupBy.summary: Groups values by category labels and aggregates each group using the specified method.

groupedData.signature: func groupedData(by categories: [String], using method: AggregationMethod) -> [(category: String, value: Element)]
groupedData.summary: Groups values by category and returns chart-ready tuples sorted by category name.

hannWindowed.summary: Applies a Hann window to reduce spectral leakage before computing the Fourier transform.

hasIntercept.summary: Whether this model includes an intercept (bias) term.

head.summary: Returns the first rows of the panel as a formatted table.

heatmapData.signature: func heatmapData(labels: [String]) -> [(x: String, y: String, value: Double)]
heatmapData.summary: Computes a correlation matrix and flattens it into labeled tuples for heatmap rendering.

histogram.signature: func histogram(bins: Int) -> [(midpoint: Element, count: Int)] where Element : BinaryFloatingPoint
histogram.summary: Divides data into evenly spaced bins and counts the frequency of values in each.

identity.summary: Creates an identity matrix of size n × n.

imbalanceRatio.summary: Returns the ratio of the largest class to the smallest class.

inertia.summary: Sum of squared distances from each point to its assigned centroid.

info.summary: Returns a statistical summary of a 2D matrix including shape, size, type, and aggregate statistics computed across all elements.

init.signature: init(_ value: Double, maxDenominator: Int = 1000, tolerance: Double = 1e-9)
init.summary: Converts a Double to its simplest fractional representation using continued fraction expansion.

inverted.summary: Returns the inverse of a square matrix.

iris.summary: 150 rows, 5 columns. Classification. Label column: `species` (encoded alphabetically as setosa→0, versicolor→1, virginica→2).

isEqual.summary: Checks if this array is equal to another array element-wise - Parameter other: The array to compare with - Returns: An array of boolean values indicating equality at each position

isGreaterThan.summary: Checks if each element in this array is greater than the specified value - Parameter value: The value to compare against - Returns: An array of boolean values

isGreaterThanOrEqual.summary: Checks if each element in this array is greater than or equal to the specified value - Parameter value: The value to compare against - Returns: An array of boolean values

isLessThan.summary: Checks if each element in this array is less than the specified value - Parameter value: The value to compare against - Returns: An array of boolean values

isLessThanOrEqual.summary: Checks if each element in this array is less than or equal to the specified value - Parameter value: The value to compare against - Returns: An array of boolean values

iterations.summary: The number of iterations the algorithm ran before stopping.

joined.signature: func joined<Separator>(separator: Separator) -> JoinedSequence<Self> where Separator : Sequence, Separator.Element == Self.Element.Element
joined.summary: Returns the concatenated elements of this sequence of sequences, inserting the given separator between each element.

k.summary: The number of neighbors to consider for each prediction.

label.summary: The predicted class label for this group.

labels.summary: The cluster label assigned to each training sample (0 to k-1).

lazy.summary: A sequence containing the same elements as this sequence, but on which some operations, such as `map` and `filter`, are implemented lazily.

lexicographicallyPrecedes.signature: func lexicographicallyPrecedes<OtherSequence>(_ other: OtherSequence, by areInIncreasingOrder: (Self.Element, Self.Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> Bool where OtherSequence : Sequence, Self.Element == OtherSequence.Element
lexicographicallyPrecedes.summary: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the sequence precedes another sequence in a lexicographical (dictionary) ordering, using the given predicate to compare elements.

LinearRegression.summary: A trained ordinary least squares regression model.

linspace.signature: static func linspace(_ start: Element, _ stop: Element, num: Int) -> [Element]
linspace.summary: Creates evenly spaced numbers over a specified closed interval.

load.signature: public static func load(path: String) -> Dataset?
load.summary: Loads a `Dataset` from any CSV file on disk.

localizedDescription.summary: Retrieve the localized description for this error.

log.summary: Returns the natural logarithm of each element in the array.

log10.summary: Returns the base-10 logarithm of each element in the array.

logAbsValue.summary: The natural logarithm of the absolute determinant value

logDeterminant.summary: Returns the sign and natural logarithm of the absolute determinant.

LogDeterminant.summary: Represents the sign and natural logarithm of a matrix determinant.

magnitude.summary: Calculates the magnitude (length) of the vector.

makeIterator.summary: Returns an iterator over the data points in this classification group.

map.signature: func map<T, E>(_ transform: (Self.Element) throws(E) -> T) throws(E) -> [T] where E : Error
map.summary: Returns an array containing the results of mapping the given closure over the sequence's elements.

masked.summary: Returns elements where the mask is true (like NumPy's array[mask]) - Parameter mask: The boolean mask to apply - Returns: Array containing only elements where the mask is true

maskedWithIndices.signature: func maskedWithIndices(by mask: [Bool]) -> [(index: Int, value: Element)]
maskedWithIndices.summary: Returns elements where the mask is true, along with their original indices.

MatrixError.summary: Errors thrown by matrix operations that can fail at runtime.

MatrixError.notSquare.summary: The operation requires a square matrix but received a non-square one.

MatrixError.singular.summary: The matrix is singular (determinant = 0) and cannot be inverted.

max.signature: @warn_unqualified_access func max(by areInIncreasingOrder: (Self.Element, Self.Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> Self.Element?
max.summary: Returns the maximum element in the sequence, using the given predicate as the comparison between elements.

maximums.summary: Per-column maximum values learned from the training data.

mean.summary: Returns the arithmetic mean (average) of all elements in the array.

means.summary: Per-column mean values learned from the training data.

meanSquaredError.summary: Mean squared error comparing these predicted values against actuals.

meanVector.summary: Computes the element-wise mean across multiple vectors, producing a single vector where each dimension is the average of the corresponding dimensions across all inputs.

median.summary: Returns the median (middle value) of the array.

metric.summary: The distance metric used to compare samples.

min.signature: @warn_unqualified_access func min(by areInIncreasingOrder: (Self.Element, Self.Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> Self.Element?
min.summary: Returns the minimum element in the sequence, using the given predicate as the comparison between elements.

minimums.summary: Per-column minimum values learned from the training data.

model.summary: The trained model that produces predictions from scaled features.

multiply.summary: Element-wise multiplication of two matrices (Hadamard product) - Parameter other: The second matrix - Returns: A new matrix where each element is the product of corresponding elements

multiplyMatrix.summary: Multiplies this matrix by another matrix to compose transformations.

name.summary: Short identifier — `"iris"`, `"titanic"`, etc., or for `load(path:)`, the input filename without its extension.

normalized.summary: Returns a normalized version of the vector (unit vector).

not.summary: Returns the element-wise logical negation of the boolean array.

ones.summary: Creates a 1D array filled with ones.

or.summary: Performs element-wise logical OR with another boolean array.

orthogonalComponent.summary: Returns the component of this vector that is perpendicular to another vector.

outlierMask.signature: func outlierMask(threshold: Element = 2.0, mean: Element? = nil, std: Element? = nil) -> [Bool]
outlierMask.summary: Detects outliers using the z-score method and returns a boolean mask.

oversample.summary: Balances an imbalanced dataset by generating synthetic samples for smaller classes.

padded.summary: Zero-pads the signal to the next power of two, satisfying the Fourier transform's input requirement.

Panel.summary: A Quiver type that organizes named columns of numeric data into a single container.

percentChange.summary: Calculates the percentage change between each element and the element a fixed number of periods before it.

percentile.signature: func percentile(_ p: Double) -> Element? where Element : BinaryFloatingPoint
percentile.summary: Calculates the value at a specific percentile using linear interpolation.

percentileRank.summary: Calculates the percentile rank of a specific value within the array.

percentileRanks.summary: Calculates the percentile rank for every value in the array.

Pipeline.signature: struct Pipeline<Model> where Model : Decodable, Model : Encodable, Model : Equatable, Model : Sendable
Pipeline.summary: A matched pair of scaler and model that travel together.

points.summary: The input data points assigned to this class.

power.summary: Raises each element to the specified power.

precision.summary: Of all predicted positives, what fraction were actually positive: TP / (TP + FP).

predict.summary: Predicts class labels for the given feature vectors. - Parameter features: 2D array where each row is a sample to classify. - Returns: An array of predicted class labels, one per sample.

predictLogProbabilities.summary: Returns log-probabilities for each class for each sample.

prefix.summary: Returns a sequence, up to the specified maximum length, containing the initial elements of the sequence.

prior.summary: Prior probability P(class), computed as count / totalSamples.

product.summary: Returns the product of all elements in the array.

quartiles.signature: func quartiles() -> (min: Element, q1: Element, median: Element, q3: Element, max: Element, iqr: Element)? where Element : BinaryFloatingPoint
quartiles.summary: Calculates the five-number summary and interquartile range of the data.

Quiver.summary: Quiver: Vector mathematics, numerical computing, and machine learning for Swift

random.signature: static func random(_ count: Int, in range: ClosedRange<Double>) -> [Double]
random.summary: Creates a 1D array of random values uniformly distributed in the given range.

randomNormal.signature: static func randomNormal(_ count: Int, mean: Double = 0.0, std: Double = 1.0) -> [Double]
randomNormal.summary: Creates a 1D array of random values from a normal (Gaussian) distribution.

range.summary: The target range that scaled values are mapped to.

recall.summary: Of all actual positives, what fraction did the model catch: TP / (TP + FN).

reduce.signature: func reduce<Result>(_ initialResult: Result, _ nextPartialResult: (Result, Self.Element) throws -> Result) rethrows -> Result
reduce.summary: Returns the result of combining the elements of the sequence using the given closure.

Regressor.summary: A protocol for supervised regression models that predict continuous values.

reshaped.summary: Reshapes a one-dimensional array into a two-dimensional matrix with the specified dimensions using row-major order.

reversed.summary: Returns an array containing the elements of this sequence in reverse order.

rollingMean.summary: Calculates a rolling mean (moving average) over a specified window.

rootMeanSquaredError.summary: Root mean squared error comparing these predicted values against actuals.

round.summary: Returns the rounded value of each element in the array.

rounded.summary: Returns each element rounded to a specified number of decimal places.

rowCount.summary: Number of rows (shared across all columns).

rSquared.summary: Coefficient of determination R² comparing these predicted values against actuals.

scalarProjection.summary: Calculates the scalar projection of this vector onto another vector.

scaled.summary: Scales values to a target range using min-max normalization.

scaler.summary: The scaler that normalizes raw inputs before prediction.

shape.signature: var shape: (rows: Int, columns: Int) { get }
shape.summary: Returns the shape of a two-dimensional array as a `(rows, columns)` tuple.

shuffled.signature: func shuffled<T>(using generator: inout T) -> [Self.Element] where T : RandomNumberGenerator
shuffled.summary: Returns the elements of the sequence, shuffled using the given generator as a source for randomness.

sigmoid.summary: Applies the sigmoid function to each element: σ(x) = 1 / (1 + e⁻ˣ).

sign.summary: The sign of the determinant: -1, 0, or 1

sin.summary: Returns the sine of each element in the array.

sineWave.signature: static func sineWave(frequency: Double, sampleRate: Double, count: Int, amplitude: Double = 1.0, offset: Double = 0.0) -> [Double]
sineWave.summary: Generates a sine wave signal at a given frequency and sample rate.

size.summary: Returns the total number of elements across all dimensions.

softmax.summary: Converts raw scores into a probability distribution that sums to 1.0.

softMax.summary: Converts raw scores into a probability distribution that sums to 1.0.

sorted.signature: func sorted<S, Comparator>(using comparators: S) -> [Self.Element] where S : Sequence, Comparator : SortComparator, Comparator == S.Element, Self.Element == Comparator.Compared
sorted.summary: Returns the elements of the sequence, sorted using the given array of `SortComparator`s to compare elements.

sortedIndices.summary: Returns the indices that would sort the array in ascending order.

split.signature: func split(maxSplits: Int = Int.max, omittingEmptySubsequences: Bool = true, whereSeparator isSeparator: (Self.Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> [ArraySlice<Self.Element>]
split.summary: Returns the longest possible subsequences of the sequence, in order, that don't contain elements satisfying the given predicate. Elements that are used to split the sequence are not returned as part of any subsequence.

sqrt.summary: Returns the square root of each element in the array.

square.summary: Returns the square of each element in the array.

stackedCumulative.summary: Stacks multiple data series cumulatively so each series builds on the one below it.

stackedPercentage.summary: Converts multiple data series into percentage-based stacking where each time point sums to 100%.

standardized.summary: Standardizes values using z-score normalization so the result has a mean of 0 and a standard deviation of 1.

StandardScaler.summary: A column-wise z-score scaler for feature matrices.

starts.signature: func starts<PossiblePrefix>(with possiblePrefix: PossiblePrefix, by areEquivalent: (Self.Element, PossiblePrefix.Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> Bool where PossiblePrefix : Sequence
starts.summary: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the initial elements of the sequence are equivalent to the elements in another sequence, using the given predicate as the equivalence test.

std.summary: Returns the standard deviation of all elements in the array.

stds.summary: Per-column standard deviation values learned from the training data.

stratifiedSplit.signature: func stratifiedSplit<L>(labels: [L], testRatio: Double, seed: UInt64) -> (trainFeatures: [Element], testFeatures: [Element], trainLabels: [L], testLabels: [L]) where L : Hashable
stratifiedSplit.summary: Splits paired feature and label arrays into training and testing subsets, preserving the class distribution from the labels in both sets.

studentPerformance.summary: 395 rows, 33 columns. Regression (or classification on a thresholded `G3`). Target column: `G3` (final grade, 0–20).

subscript.summary: Returns the values for the named column as a Quiver vector.

subtract.summary: Element-wise subtraction of two matrices - Parameter other: The second matrix - Returns: A new matrix where each element is the difference of corresponding elements

suffix.summary: Returns a subsequence, up to the given maximum length, containing the final elements of the sequence.

sum.summary: Returns the sum of all elements in the array.

summary.summary: Returns per-column summary statistics as a formatted string.

tan.summary: Returns the tangent of each element in the array.

tokenize.summary: Tokenizes text into individual words for text analysis and embedding workflows.

toMatrix.summary: Extracts selected columns as a matrix.

toPanel.summary: Returns the underlying Quiver `Panel` so the rest of the Quiver API (`head()`, `summary()`, `trainTestSplit()`, `toMatrix()`, etc.) is available on the dataset's columns.

topIndices.signature: func topIndices(k: Int) -> [(index: Int, score: Double)]
topIndices.summary: Returns the indices and values of the top K highest elements.

trainingFeatures.summary: The training feature vectors, stored for distance computation at prediction time.

trainingLabels.summary: The training labels, one per feature vector.

trainTestSplit.signature: func trainTestSplit(testRatio: Double, seed: UInt64) -> (train: [Element], test: [Element])
trainTestSplit.summary: Splits the array into training and testing subsets using a seeded shuffle for reproducible results.

transform.summary: Transforms a vector by this matrix (matrix-vector multiplication)

transformedBy.summary: Transforms this vector using a matrix (matrix-vector multiplication).

transpose.summary: Returns the transpose of a matrix

transposed.summary: Returns the transpose of a matrix (convenience method matching Swift naming conventions)

trueIndices.summary: Returns the indices of all `true` elements in the boolean array.

trueNegatives.summary: Negative samples the model correctly identified as negative.

truePositives.summary: Positive samples the model correctly identified as positive.

underestimatedCount.summary: A value less than or equal to the number of elements in the sequence, calculated nondestructively.

value.summary: Reconstructs the determinant value (sign times exp of log absolute value)

variance.summary: Returns the variance of all elements in the array.

variances.summary: Variance of each feature for samples in this class.

vectorProjection.summary: Calculates the vector projection of this vector onto another vector.

version.summary: Library version. Keep in sync with the package tag at release time.

VoteWeight.summary: The weighting strategy for neighbor votes in K-Nearest Neighbors.

VoteWeight.distance.summary: Closer neighbors get more influence: weight = 1 / distance.

VoteWeight.uniform.summary: Each neighbor gets one vote regardless of distance.

weight.summary: The weighting strategy for neighbor votes.

withContiguousStorageIfAvailable.signature: func withContiguousStorageIfAvailable<R>(_ body: (UnsafeBufferPointer<Self.Element>) throws -> R) rethrows -> R?
withContiguousStorageIfAvailable.summary: Executes a closure on the sequence’s contiguous storage.

zeros.summary: Creates a 1D array filled with zeros.
